poem.content:
In the town where the Solana sun sets low,
A gate stands tall, a portal to our show,
A place where dreams and dust entwine,
And hopes are forged, like a craft divine.

We gather 'round, an animal heart,
A community that never will start
To deny the beauty that we bring,
A smile on every face, like a morning wing.

We hold up hands, in unity we swear,
A globe above us, with no care or fear,
From the top of the hill, we gaze and behold,
A view of a world, where love is the gold.

In the old recipe books, a story unfolds,
Of a people who put love into every fold,
A blouse, like a leaf, rustles in the tree,
A symbol of hope, in this world of humanity.

But in a small case, a secret would reside,
A memory of the past, where love would not divide,
A cupboard of tears, where stories of old,
Echo through time, in whispers that never grow cold.

In a brick shed, a camp was once set up high,
Where laughter and joy, did reach up to the sky,
But now it seems, a distant, fading light,
A remnant of happiness, in the dark of night.

Yet still we hold on, to what we once had found,
A sense of community, that was never lost, or drowned,
In the depths of our hearts, a spark still glows bright,
A happy memory, that guides us through the darkest night.
